calendar.matters:
MOTION TO SET ASIDE DEFAULT/DEFAULT JUDGMENT/LEAVE TO DEFEND
Off Calendar Feb-06-2015 Denied
MINI MINUTES:
APPEARANCES: Atty. J. BRYCE KENNY, Tel No. (707) 442-4431 appearing for Defendant Bryan Lamb;
No other appearances made in this matter.

RULING - Matter was not argued. The Court adopted its tentative ruling. DEFENDANT BRYAN LAMB'S MOTION TO SET ASIDE DEFAULT JUDGMENT is denied. The time for bringing a motion pursuant to CCP 473(b) has expired. Thus, the Court may only set aside the default judgment by using its powers of equity if Defendant establishes "extrinsic fraud or mistake." (Aldrich v. San Fernando Valley Lumber Co. (1985) 170 Cal.App.3d 725, 738.) Defendant's "own negligence in following up and pursuing his case" should also be scrutinized. (Id. at 740.) The Declaration of Bryan Lamb shows that Defendant was negligent in pursuing his case, and as such, is not entitled to equitable relief. For example, Defendant states that the parties agreed that Mr. Jensen was hired to pursue settlement and attend a case management conference, not for all purposes. (Declaration of Bryan Lamb, par. 3.) Defendant also states that Mr. Jensen and Defendant were unable to agree on a time to meet and communications broke down when Defendant was unable to receive calls due to failure to pay his phone bill. (Id., par. 6.) Once communications broke down, Lamb "assumed that [Mr. Jensen] had persuaded the other side not to pursue the case, and they had dropped out." (Id., par. 7.) Defendant also states that he received some letters from Mr. Jensen but does not recall what they stated. (Id.) The evidence shows that Defendant did not take the necessary steps to ensure his case was being properly handled, and therefore, Defendant is not entitled to equitable relief. PLAINTIFF'S PROPOSED ORDER SIGNED IN OPEN COURT. Judge: Ernest H. Goldsmith; Clerk: Sean Kane; Reporter: Melanie Dawn Gheno, CSR #7489 (302/EHG)

No appearance on behalf or by defaulted defendant BRYAN LAMB, SAN FRANCISCO URBAN RETAIL CORPORATION.

After evidence, Court ordered the following Judgment entered in favor of LEILA GOWEN and against Defendant BRYAN LAMB, SAN FRANCISCO URBAN RETAIL as follows:
Principal $20,000.00
Punitive damages $8,000.00
Pre-Judgment Interest $5,531.54
Attorney's Fees $17,682.00
Costs $1,192.00
Total Judgment in the amount of $52,405.54
Judgment signed in open court.
